description_long: This is a pair of adjustable groove-joint pliers, commonly known as channel lock pliers, featuring two elongated, slightly curved handles coated in a greenish rubber or plastic for improved grip and comfort. The jaws are offset and have a distinctive curved, serrated gripping surface, designed to securely hold pipes, bolts, or irregularly shaped objects. The head is metallic, showing signs of wear and oxidation, and includes a slip-joint mechanism that allows the jaw width to be adjusted for different tasks. The pivot point is prominent and reinforced, enabling the handles to move smoothly while maintaining strong clamping force. The handles are robust and ergonomically shaped, tapering slightly towards the ends for better leverage. No visible branding or logo is present on the tool.
description: A pair of green-handled, adjustable groove-joint pliers with curved, serrated jaws and a slip-joint mechanism. The tool is metallic with worn surfaces and ergonomic, rubber-coated handles.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pliers, highlighting the green handles and the curved, serrated jaws.
description_view_1: Front-facing view emphasizing the open jaws and the alignment of the handles, with the slip-joint mechanism partially visible.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the parallel alignment of the handles and the narrow profile of the jaws.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the handles and the pivot area, with the jaws partially obscured.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, elongated handles with green rubber or plastic insulation for ergonomic grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Curved, serrated jaws with a groove-joint design for adjustable width, optimized for gripping round or irregular objects.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Slip-joint pivot mechanism allowing adjustable jaw width with a visible reinforced pin.
attribute_grip_pattern: Rubberized handles with a smooth, slightly textured surface for enhanced grip.
